channel.title: Meta on Leyline — Demo
channel.description: Recent content in Meta on Leyline — Demo

item.title: Welcome to Leyline
item.description:
Leyline is a minimal Hugo theme aimed at writing. It does a few things well:

  • Sticky header with a logo, menu, and quick controls.
  • A light/dark switcher that respects your OS preference until you choose otherwise.
  • A quiet home page that surfaces featured posts (or your latest, if you haven’t picked any).

Why “Leyline”?

The name nods to invisible threads of structure — the kind of design you don’t notice because everything is where you’d expect.

item.title: Building Leyline in the open
item.description:
Most themes I’ve used arrived as finished artifacts. Polished, pre-decided. This time I wanted to do it differently: think out loud, pick at the trade-offs, and keep the small commits that don’t usually make it past someone’s local branch.

The shape of Leyline reflects that. The sticky header didn’t need three iterations to land; the second one was right. But the home page took a while: featured posts vs. latest posts vs. a third “evergreen” section that I tried and then quietly deleted. You don’t see that deletion in the diff. You see the result.
